Article.ArticleTitle: Single-atom-enhanced molecularly imprinted electrochemical sensor with Fe3O4-Ru1@NC nanocatalysts for ultrasensitive detection of 5-heneicosylresorcinol in whole wheat food.

Abstract.AbstractText: Accurate quantification of alkylresorcinols is crucial for evaluating the nutritional quality and authenticity of whole wheat products, yet current analytical methods often lack sensitivity and practicality. In this study, we developed a novel electrochemical sensor that integrates single-atom catalysis (Fe3O4-Ru1@NC nanocatalysts) with molecularly imprintedpolymers (MIPs) for the sensitive and selective detection of 5-heneicosylresorcinol (AR21), a key alkylresorcinol homolog. For the first time, a facile electropolymerization approach was employed to construct AR21-specific MIPs, enabling highly selective molecular recognition, while the incorporation of Fe3O4-Ru1@NC significantly enhanced the sensing sensitivity. The obtained sensor exhibited a wide linear range of 0.1-100 μg·mL-1 and an ultralow detection limit of 71 ng·mL-1, along with excellent selectivity and stability. Analysis of real whole-wheat samples demonstrated high accuracy (91.40-108.50% recovery), showing good agreement with standard HPLC measurements. This sensing platform shows great potential for routine quality control of whole-grain products and may future advances in food analysis.
